claim.statement: Interactions should feel responsive: target an Interaction to Next Paint (INP) of 200 ms or less at the 75th percentile, the 'good' threshold for this Core Web Vital. Keep input delay, event processing, and presentation work short so the UI paints quickly after user input.

limitations: INP is a field metric measured at p75; 200ms is the good/needs-improvement boundary, 500ms the poor boundary

do: Break up long tasks and yield to the main thread between work units
Defer non-urgent work until after the next paint
Minimise event-handler work and avoid synchronous layout in handlers
avoid: Heavy synchronous JavaScript on click/keydown/tap handlers
Large, monolithic main-thread tasks that block rendering

strategies: memoise and offload to web workers
